_note: Pre-computed insight cards for the AI Insights panel. The 'AI' label here means 'analyst-curated insight'; no live model call is made. Numbers are derived from summary.json (Chukalla et al., Nature Food 2025).

cards.title: Crop water use rose 9% in a decade — almost entirely from rainfall
cards.body: Global crop water consumption climbed from 6,132 km³ in 2010 to 6,668 km³ in 2020. Green (rainfall) water grew +9.8%, while blue (irrigation) water grew only +3.5%. The decade's expansion in agricultural water demand was driven almost entirely by rainfed land, not by new irrigation.

cards.title: Half of global irrigation water serves just two cereals
cards.body: In 2020, rice consumed 328 km³ of blue (irrigation) water and wheat consumed 212 km³ — together 540 km³, or 50% of the world's 1,080 km³ agricultural blue-water footprint. Rice alone is responsible for 30% of all blue water consumed by crops globally.
evidence: Rice: 328 km³ blue (30.4% of global blue)
Wheat: 212 km³ blue (19.7% of global blue)

cards.title: Rainfed agriculture is the silent majority
cards.stat_label: of all crop water is green (rainfall)
cards.body: In 2020, 84% of crop water consumption came from soil moisture (green water), and only 16% from irrigation (blue water). This share has stayed remarkably stable from 2000 to 2020, despite irrigation expansion — meaning rainfed systems are absorbing the bulk of growing demand.
evidence: Rainfed land: 1,115 Mha (79% of cropped area)
Irrigated land: 292 Mha but 38% of production

cards.title: Water productivity dropped between 2010 and 2020
cards.body: Global water productivity — production per unit of crop water — fell from 1.28 kg/m³ in 2010 to 1.16 kg/m³ in 2020. Even though area grew 8% and water use grew 9%, total production was essentially flat (−1.2%). The decade's expansion did not translate into proportional output.

cards.title: Maize, soybean and cassava lead the decade's growth
cards.body: Three crops expanded their water footprint sharply between 2010 and 2020: maize green water +23%, soybean green water +18%, and cassava green water +48%. All three growth stories are rainfall-driven — none required matching irrigation expansion.

cards.title: Oil palm 2000 figure looks discontinuous — flagged for review
cards.body: Oil palm appears to drop from 638 km³ in 2000 to 179 km³ in 2010 before partially recovering to 303 km³ in 2020. This is almost certainly a coverage / classification change in the upstream SPAM crop layer rather than real biophysical change. Treat the 2000 oil-palm value with caution; the 2010 → 2020 comparison is the trustworthy interval.
evidence: Many other crops also have missing/sparse 2000 values
